candidates.stmt: I am standing for this ward because I believe that there needs to be a different voice in local politics. The Green Party stands for that change and I want to be part of developing Aldridge Central and South. I want to protect the Green Belt and protect the local enviornment that has a positive impact on the lives of the people in our ward.

"I’m standing as your Reform UK candidate because I believe every resident in Beechdale, Leamore, and Reedswood has a fundamental right to feel safe in their own home and on their own street. Right now, that isn't happening. We’ve been left to deal with the fallout of 'soft-touch' policing and a council that has lost its grip on our local hotspots.
I’m here to be a friendly face for our law-abiding neighbours, but a nightmare for those making our lives a misery.
If you vote for me, I will:
Reclaim Stephenson Square: This area shouldn’t be a 'no-go' zone for our seniors or families. I will fight for a permanent police 'pod' or a regular, fixed-time mobile station until the gangs and bike-nuisance are cleared for good.
Stop the Reedswood School Plan: Safety isn't just about crime; it's about our environment. I will fight the plan to build on Reedswood Park. Destroying 14 acres of our green space will bring chaos to our roads, making them less safe for our children and causing gridlock for residents. We need to protect our woodland, not pave over it.
End the 'Fly-Tipping' Arson Cycle: We’ve seen the car fires and the rubbish blazes near the canal. This isn't just a mess; it's a danger to our homes. I will demand the installation of high-quality CCTV in known dumping spots and push for immediate prosecution of those setting fires in our ward.
Demand Common-Sense Policing: Reform UK is about getting officers out from behind desks and onto the pavements. I’ll be a constant thorn in the side of the Police and Crime Commissioner to ensure our ward gets its fair share of patrols—especially in the evenings around the Wyrley Estate and Reedswood Close.
Protect our Streets from Speeders: Our residential roads, like Remington Road, aren't racetracks. I’ll fight for community-led speed watches and proper traffic calming measures that actually work, rather than just more revenue-raising cameras.
The 'old' parties have had decades to fix this and failed. I’m not interested in excus

I grew up in the Bentley & Darlaston North Ward - I know the area well and understand the concern that the ward has been left behind, suffered from chronic underinvestment and there are alarming issues in relation to youth opportunity and security for our elders.

I am a practising solicitor specialising in Family and Criminal Law with a decade long experience in local government; I have also served as a School Governor at Wood Green School since 2015. I will use my experience and best endeavours to push for greater investment and more services in the area.

I’m Louis Gill. I began my teaching career in Darlaston, helping young people build confidence and skills for life. With roots in industry and a commitment to real opportunity, I’m standing to get things done and put Bentley and Darlaston North first.

‘I am proud to be standing for re-election as Councillor for Bentley & Darlaston North. I’ve worked hard to support residents with local issues, including anti-social behaviour on King Street and Bentley Common. I’ve represented all residents at full council meetings. Additionally I co-organised events such as the 2025 Darlaston Christmas event, DARLO DAY 2026 and Remembrance Day Parades. I remain committed to delivering for local residents".
. - Cllr Lucie Nahal

The people of my ward will always come first 
Born in blakenall , first family home in lower farm ,
Now live in Bloxwich east next to Bloxwich it’s self , 
I know the people , I know the town and village 
It stays deep in my heart 
I will be upfront to all if I see a decline in my town 
And challenge every challenge that comes my way, 
Make my self accountable and accessible to people’s needs and voice there concerns 
Help reduced Walsall debt off 33000 a day in interest 
Keep frontline jobs protected 
Help Walsall has a whole thrive , with the help of all reform candidates and the elected 
We are the only the party with common sense and may it prevail 
Conservatives have had there chance 
Labour are seen for who they are 
Greens policies are unpredictable 
Trust a reform lead Walsall council 
God bless all

It's time to return politics and decisions back  to the people who feel the impact of them. To make sure that our decisions are aware of the impact they have, and to make these decisions in the need of the many, the person on the street.

I'm a local counsellor, manager in the local drug and alcohol support service, dad, musician, fitness focused individual who wants to help make our future brighter.

I promise to make decisions and focus on what is the best for us today, tomorrow and in 10 years time. To cut through the noise, the distractions and propaganda, to provide for the proud people of Walsall who deserve a future.

The best is yet to come.

The two party system isn’t working, we need and deserve some real change which only Reform UK can offer. I support local businesses and I want to make improvements along the Pelsall Road and Clayhanger Lane. I live in Brownhills and I want to help our community thrive again.

As a Brownhills councillor, I will fight for there to be a local police office open to the public where police officers can be deployed from for the local area. Creating swifter responses and police presence to deter anti social behaviour and theft. I will work to use funding for ravens court and other run down and closed off buildings in our area. We want to bring back a market for our visitors and locals for choice and vibrancy. We would like community centres for our young and old to use for projects and activities. Let’s bring safety and pride back to our Brownhills; enabling it to thrive once more.

Hiya, my name is Kathryn Smith and I'm your Green Party candidate for Brownhills.

I moved to Walsall in 1999 and I've lived in Brownhills for nearly 8 years and absolutely love the community here. I enjoy walking my dogs along The McClean Way and around our beautiful green spaces. I have dedicated my working life to helping others, this includes youth and family work, delivering education in our local schools when I worked for Street Teams & Nacro. My heart has always been to help anyone in need in my community and to bring a smile.

Today, I work as a Celebrant, bringing love, kindness, and support to grieving families during life’s hardest times.  When I'm not working I am an active member of a vibrant church in Walsall Town centre, I think God is pretty awesome.  I'm also a dedicated community litter picker, committed to keeping Brownhills litter free. In a nut shell, I believe in a future for our town built on caring for one another, investing into small businesses, and looking after our green spaces. I promise to speak on your behalf and help with local issues.  💚 Vote for a local voice.
Walsall Independent Group.

Helen Neville is standing as the Green Party candidate because she believes local politics should work for the people who live here, not for the system itself. She knows Walsall residents face real pressures: stretched services, housing problems, road safety concerns, and local streets and green spaces that don’t get the care they deserve. These are not inevitable - they are the result of decisions that fail to put people first. Helen is ready to challenge that.

Mish Rahman is a local resident and community organiser who has lived and worked in Walsall all his life. Mish is standing as the Green Party candidate for Paddock to represent the residents of Paddock because he recognises that locally things are no longer working for ordinary people.

After years of cuts to local services and rising council tax,residents are paying more while getting less in return.

Streets are dirtier, housing repairs take longer, support services are stretched. People are ignored once the election is over.

Mish believes this isn’t inevitable, it's the result of political choices. Labour and Conservative councillors argue over blame while Reform exploits that anger, building hate and division. Meanwhile, local residents are left dealing with the consequences.

Mish believes local politics has to be done differently. That means being visible, accessible and accountable, not disappearing and then reappearing at election time. If elected, he will hold regular street surgeries and open meetings, publish updates on what’s being done, and make it clear that residents don’t need connections or favours to be heard.

As a Green councillor, Mish will oppose cuts that hit our communities hardest and push for investment in services people rely on from housing and waste collection to community cohesion and support for local people. Being Green means refusing to accept cuts as normal and putting local people first.

Mish’s politics are simple: challenge inequality, stand up to unfair cuts, and make sure the council works for the people it serves.

His commitment to Paddock is to be present, to listen and to fight for a fairer Walsall where residents aren't overlooked and ignored.

Statement to Voters – Jade Chapman (Pelsall)

I live in Pelsall with my family and understand the day-to-day issues residents face, from traffic and parking pressures to road safety, anti-social behaviour, and concerns around overdevelopment.

I currently serve as a councillor for Rushall-Shelfield. Following boundary changes, parts of that ward are now moving into Pelsall. As a Pelsall resident, it makes sense for me to stand here, to represent the community I live in and see every day.

During my time as a councillor, I have worked directly with residents to resolve local issues, raise concerns with the council, and push for practical solutions. I focus on getting things done and making sure residents are properly heard.

If elected in Pelsall, my priorities will be:

Improving road safety, crossings, and parking
Tackling anti-social behaviour
Protecting green spaces and managing development responsibly
Ensuring local concerns are acted on, not ignored

Local elections are about local issues. I am standing to provide a strong, responsive voice for Pelsall.  Focused on action, accountability, and putting residents first.

I live in Pelsall and have my election literature printed in Pelsall.

The Walsall plan provides for new properties should be built should be built in Pelsall without looking at the infrastructure of the village with oversubscribed schools, doctors not to mention the already congested roads. This is WRONG!

I am against Net Zero policies such as the ultra low emission zones (ULEZ) which persecute the lowest paid in society and force them to buy vehicles they will struggle to buy due to the cost of living Crisis and Low Traffic Neighbourhood’s (LTN’s). These restrict which road and streets you can use. Whilst things do not affect the constituency at the moment they are in operation in Birmingham and Oxford.

I support Restore Britain’s plan to stop the boats and call on the  UK to leave the European Court of Human Rights and the European convention on human rights (ECHR) In order to control migration. Also the Rwanda plan is a waste of taxpayers money. Restore Britain would take the illegal migrants straight back to France. Not Rwanda.

I attend and support Freind’s of the Pelsall commons meetings
I have called in the planning application for the House of Multiple Occupancy (HMO) in Mount Road in Pelsall.

I am a social member of Pelsall Cricket and Sports club and I sponsor a coach at  Walsall Wood Football Club. If elected I will support local clubs and societies. I am a member of the Society for the Protection of Unborn Children. In addition I’m Also an Associate member of The Freedom Association.

Hello! I have lived in Walsall for my whole life and I've witnessed first-hand it's decline. But I am proud to call Walsall my home, I always will be. Pelsall is a wonderful village with thriving businesses and a great carnival. I would be delighted to be given the opportunity to represent you, the people and pride of this village. Please keep an eye out for me, I'm always up for a chat!

When local media is portraying Pelsall as the village 'that's lovely in the day but changes after 6pm', you know something isn't working.

Pelsall is a vibrant village, with local businesses dedicated to serving their community. I want to be a voice for the village, that has spent far too long being neglected by the previous councils.

I want to make Pelsall thrive.

_Priorities for Pelsall, Priorities for You:_
- **Clean up neglected buildings** and sites by fully enforcing existing powers to tackle untidy and derelict properties, making our towns and high streets safer, more attractive and more welcoming.
- **Maximise the Walsall pound** by aiming to shift at least 10% more public-sector spending to local businesses, co-ops and social enterprises generating over £100 million in wider economic benefit.
- **Introduce participatory budgeting**, allowing residents to directly allocate a portion of council capital.
- **Invest in more community protection officers** to patrol district centres and hotspot locations providing a dedicated and visible presence for safer streets.
- **Invest in festivals, cultural events and heritage sites** to celebrate Walsall’s identity and boost local tourism.

It's time for Pelsall to get the attention, time and investment it rightfully deserves.

CEO for 23 years at Ian Henery Solicitors Ltd, a multi-award winning full service law firm in Birmingham Ladywood; qualified Chartered Management Accountant; property developer and landlord; radio producer at Black Country Radio; chairperson of Walsall branch of the Society for the Protection of Unborn Children, staunch Catholic, mother of 4 daughters.

I'm honoured to have been selected as one of your local Green Party candidates for Rushall-Shelfield. If anyone has any questions for me or would like to meet up to discuss local issues, please email me.

Listening to people is key, and I'll do my best to get the people of Rushall-Shelfield they need and want. These are the things I'm passionate about when it comes to local government, and some of the things I'll be fighting for as your local Councillor:

● Put people first, more inclusive public consultations ensuring Rushall-Shelfield residents have more power in decision-making.

● Protect our green belt from private developers. New housing should be built on brownfield sites and existing empty buildings re-purposed for housing where appropriate.

● Improve our parks and green spaces, so we can continue to enjoy them, feeling safe and secure, reducing criminal activity and anti-social behaviour, and upgrading play equipment and facilities.

● Better policing in our Borough with increased visibility of officers on the street and strengthening neighbourhood watch schemes to reduce crime such as vandalism, anti-social behaviour and fly-tipping.

● Public investment in infrastructure such as waterways, transport, health, trade, leisure and tourism to ensure Pelsall and the wider Walsall borough is thriving.

● Protect local budgets, and push for increased funding for our local services.

● Scrap expensive vanity projects that do not serve the residents of Rushall-Shelfield and the wider Walsall borough, and better spend those funds in our local communities.

● Fight against council tax rises. In a cost of living crisis we need people to have more in their pockets, not less. That's how we grow the local economy.

We've had 14 years of Conservative governments in Westminster cutting budgets across the board. You can't run a country on the cheap and expect good results. Imagine cutting your household budget year on year, which many of us already are, you'd run out of fo

I am proud to be one of your Walsall Community Independents candidates for the Rushall-Shelfield ward.

National establishment parties like Labour, Reform UK, Green and the Conservatives answer to party leaders in Westminster.

We answer to you.

As Independents our decisions are based on what you tell us - not what National or Regional party leaders tell us to say. We would focus on local priorities like

* Safer Streets
* Cleaner Neighbourhoods
* Protecting the Green Belt
* Reliable local services
* Councillors who are visible, accountable and part of the community all year round.

We are here to serve you, not a mainstream national party. We are here to listen and to make sure the issues that matter to you are heard loud and clear in the Council Chamber.

Vote for myself and Walsall Community Independents for Councillors that are focused on local issues that answer to you, not national and regional party leaders.

I’m Aadam, and I am proud to represent Rushall and Shelfield as a member of the Walsall Community Independents.

As an 18-year-old, I believe I can offer a fresh perspective. I am not bound by traditional ways of doing things, and I am ready to speak out if decisions are not in the best interests of the people of Rushall and Shelfield.

As a councillor, I will serve you, not a national party, solely for your community. I am here to listen to the people of Rushall and Shelfield and ensure their voices are heard.

The Conservatives in Walsall are totally divided and out of ideas. Your vote will decide whether our town suffers years of further decline under a clapped-out Conservative run council, or a new modern fresh start under REFORM UK.  
 
The elections on 7 May 2026 really matter. These are the most important elections in over 20 years with all 60 council seats on Walsall Council up for election at the same time. You can change every councillor all in one go.  You can vote for three candidates in every ward so use your three votes to vote for all three Reform UK candidates. 
 
The Conservatives running Walsall Council have been in power for most of the last 20 years. Do you think things have got better or worse in that time? 
 
No matter who you’ve voted for in the past, if you want pride, care, honesty and integrity restored in our town, the only option is to vote for REFORM UK. These are our values.

So in Short Heath vote for myself, Elaine Williams and our other two candidates, Rohan Beecha and Bella Simon.

10 reasons to vote for us at the all-out elections on 7th May 2026
 
REFORM in Walsall will: 
 
1.	Extend brown bin garden waste collections into December. Currently collections finish too soon in early Autumn before all the leaves from the trees have fallen. 
 
2.	Deploy additional cover and overt CCTV for each ward to tackle crime, anti-social behaviour and fly-tipping. You can tell your REFORM councillor where you want cameras located in your area and we will endeavour to make it happen. 
 
3.	Expand fly-tipping and litter enforcement. We understand Walsall Council is issuing less than half the number of littering fines compared to when this was contracted out to a company called ‘Kingdom’.  REFORM will use existing powers to seize the vehicles of fly-tippers. REFORM will pursue duty of care prosecutions where the owner of the rubbish, not the tipper is fined. This will be done by searches of tipped rubbish. We understand Walsall Council has never had a duty o

Having the opportunity to volunteer in the community and in the Council, I am passionate about the rights of children and young people, mental wellbeing and youth mentoring as well as prioritising the integration of communities of ethnic minority. The change is possible. Reform is ready to transform our communities for the better.

Dr Enoch Adewunmi Oyeduntan is a behavioural change and public health communications professional, media and journalism expert, charity advocate and public service enthusiast with a strong educational background and professional experience across the public sector, Adult Social Care, youth engagement, wellbeing, fundraising, volunteering and community work.

He is passionate about listening to residents, supporting families and young people, strengthening community voices, promoting inclusion, encouraging safer neighbourhoods, and using education, charity work, media, communication and partnership building to unlock local potential and make Walsall greater.

Akhter Ali and Jamal Ahmed are ‘Independent Candidates’
They are free from Party affiliation and restrictions.They can advocate for their ward and constituents, speak up for people regardless of race, religion and ethnicity, without constraints.

Their sole aim is the rejuvenation of their ward and greater Walsall area.

Their loyalty will be to the people, not to political parties 
or any party agendas.

I am passionate about supporting the local community, listening to their needs and championing their causes in all the work I will do. 
As a teacher and school leader for two decades I am clear and confident, and I will be able to express the views of residents clearly and confidently to Council officers.
I will use my experience of advocating for people to deal with issues as they arise but also use my opportunity when in the Council chamber to keep Willenhall South on the agenda. 
I am also already working closely with your two sitting Labour Councillors and together we will continue to improve the area for all.
